diary:
Woke up in Sammamish before the sun really got going, coffee in one hand and my route options in the other. The Light Rail closure between Westlake and University District had me double-checking everything, and I’m glad I did—traffic felt oddly busy even before I hit the road. By 7:07 I was headed into the Issaquah work area, picking up tools and confirming the turbine site schedule like it was a ritual.

The morning stretched with inspections and safety checks, then the afternoon too—steady work, the kind where you can’t rush the details. Around lunch I ended up at Pike Place Market and ate outside, letting the warm air soak in between paperwork and photos. Later I grabbed water and snacks at QFC on Capitol Hill, then drove back out for more repairs and crew coordination.

Downtown felt alive waiting for USA vs Wales at the Triple Door—laughs, the clink of glasses, and that World Cup buzz in the air. Afterward I wandered near Gas Works Park, caught the sunset, and ended up scribbling a few lines for open-mic poetry back at home. I feel tired, but good—like the day made sense.
thought_process: I planned my day around the predictable stuff—turbine maintenance is always time-sensitive, so I wanted the morning commute to be smooth and tool-ready before any delays piled up. The Light Rail closure pushed me to favor driving and to confirm schedules early, especially since the afternoon site location required a clean route. I chose Pike Place Market for lunch because being outside mattered today; the weather (sunny, mid-70s) made me less likely to feel drained during the second round of inspections. The World Cup match was an easy mood anchor—USA vs Wales at 11am PDT meant I could decompress without committing to an all-day downtown crowd. Seeing other fans at The Triple Door helped me shake off work tension, and walking near the water afterward reset my head for the poetry and camping checklist. My mood started practical, warmed up with the sun, then settled into calm by night.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
